decision: retain sparse c31 identity scheduling as a candidate-filter mechanism only if the frozen <=268-switch material gate passes; the stricter <=252 stretch gate decides whether the current order itself is already efficient enough
hypothesis: wrong complete 256-bit candidates can be rejected by a sparse subset of the 336 c31 majority identities missing from the depth-30 relaxation

mechanism.adaptive_picker: online lexicographic immediate propagation gain from public output plus the complete candidate being filtered; no target key or branching
mechanism.base_relation: all XOR gates and c1..c30 majority recurrences exact; one independent c31 Boolean remains in each of 336 additions
mechanism.certificate: an empty local truth-table domain after enabling an ordered prefix; a recorded reason-DAG slice is replayed independently as the exact wrong-candidate rejection certificate
mechanism.materiality_rule_frozen_before_run: proof-replayed conflict at <=268 switches, leaving at least 68/336 identities absent; <=252 is retained as a 25%-omission stretch gate
mechanism.propagation: deterministic incremental generalized arc consistency with no branching
mechanism.switch: c31 = majority(a30,b30,c30) for one named addition

online_candidate_public_gain_greedy.reason: the deterministic next switch is recomputed from each candidate's current exact propagation state

summary.success_gate: at least one exact proof-replayed wrong-probe conflict at <=268 identities
